name: Meta Infer Static Analyzer
what_it_does: Open-source static analysis tool from Meta (Facebook) — enabling developers to find null pointer dereferences, resource leaks, concurrency races, and memory errors in Java, C, C++, Objective-C, and Kotlin code, using abstract interpretation for deep interprocedural analysis, particularly strong for Android Java and iOS Objective-C/Swift, with no API service (CLI-only tool).
use_cases: Detecting null pointer dereferences and resource leaks in Android apps via Infer in CI/CD
Finding concurrency bugs (data races, deadlocks) in Java code via Infer analysis
Scanning iOS Objective-C/Swift for memory management errors via Infer
Integrating Infer JSON results into CI/CD pipeline quality gates for Java projects
Detecting file/cursor/database resource leaks via Infer's resource leak checker
not_for: Enterprise SAST with centralized management (use Coverity or Klocwork for team-scale SAST)
Security vulnerability scanning with CVE mapping (Infer is bug detection, not security tool)
Languages outside Java, C/C++, Objective-C, Kotlin/Swift
best_when: An Android or Java/Kotlin team needs deep interprocedural static analysis for null safety and resource leaks — Infer's bi-abduction algorithm catches bugs that simpler tools miss.
avoid_when: You need enterprise policy management, CVE database integration, or multi-language coverage beyond Meta's supported languages.

auth.notes: No authentication required. CLI tool — no API service. Run locally: infer run -- javac MyCode.java or infer run -- clang -c file.c. Output: infer-out/ directory with bugs.txt, report.json. No network calls. GitHub Actions integration via action. No REST API — purely local analysis tool.

pricing.notes: Created by Meta (Facebook) Research. MIT license. GitHub: facebook/infer (14,000+ stars). Used extensively by Meta for Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp code. Abstract interpretation based (Bi-Abduction algorithm — research innovation). Supported languages: Java, C, C++, Objective-C, Kotlin (experimental). Special checkers: Null Dereference, Resource Leak, Memory Leak (C/C++), Deadlock, Data Race (RacerD), SIOF (Static Initialization Order Fiasco), Biabduction. Slower than pattern-matching tools — analysis can take minutes/hours for large codebases. Docker image available: ghcr.io/facebook/infer. Incremental analysis supported. Integration: Gradle, Maven, Buck, Xcode.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: CLI tool only — no API. MIT license. No auth. JSON/text output. Java/C/C++/Obj-C. Abstract interpretation (slow but deep). Meta research origin. Docker available.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Analysis is deterministic for same source code. Results in infer-out/report.json. Fully idempotent for same input.
known_agent_gotchas: No REST API — purely local CLI tool, not a network service
infer run -- [build command]: wraps build command to intercept compilation
Analysis is slow (abstract interpretation): large Java projects may take 30+ minutes
Results in infer-out/report.json — parse JSON for CI integration
Incremental mode: infer run --reactive (only re-analyzes changed files)
Docker: docker run --rm -v $PWD:/code ghcr.io/facebook/infer infer run -- javac ...

security_score_components.security_notes: No credentials required — zero auth attack surface. MIT open source — fully auditable. Offline execution — no code leaves developer machine. Meta research provenance — academic-grade analysis. Abstract interpretation provides deep, sound analysis. Java/Android: catches null pointer and resource leaks before production. No data leaves local environment.
